event: Views of Petra — Eastern End of the Valley
symbols: rock-cut facades — the vanished grandeur of Edom/Nabataea, fulfilling prophetic oracles of desolation
towering sandstone cliffs — the immovable natural fortress that Obadiah addressed: 'you who dwell in the clefts of the rock'
small human figures dwarfed by ruins — the transience of human civilization against the permanence of God's word
camels and travelers — the ongoing passage of peoples through lands once mighty but now desolate
description: A warm-toned landscape showing the ancient ruins of Petra carved into massive sandstone cliffs. The left side is dominated by an elaborate rock-cut facade with columns, pediments, and doorways hewn directly from the rose-red cliff face, rising several stories high. Additional carved tombs and chambers are visible along the cliff wall stretching to the right. Enormous boulders and natural rock formations tower above and around the ruins. Small figures — travelers and camels — are scattered on the sandy valley floor below, giving scale to the monumental architecture.
theological_description: Petra, the ancient Nabataean capital, is almost certainly identifiable with the biblical Sela (meaning 'rock') in the territory of Edom. The prophets pronounced devastating oracles against Edom for its pride and hostility toward Israel. Obadiah 1:3-4 addresses Edom directly: 'The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high.' Isaiah 34:10-15 prophesies that Edom will become a perpetual desolation. The ruins of Petra, once a thriving metropolis now empty and inhabited only by occasional travelers, stand as a visible fulfillment of these prophetic words. David Roberts's 19th-century documentation of the site brought these biblical connections to European consciousness, making Petra a touchstone for the veracity of biblical prophecy in the Victorian imagination.

scripture_refs.reason: The pride of your heart has deceived you, you who dwell in the clefts of the rock — oracle against Edom/Petra
Edom shall become a perpetual desolation — prophetic oracle fulfilled in Petra's abandonment
Amaziah captured Sela — possible biblical reference to Petra itself

_meta.title: Pelra. Views of the Eastern End of the Valley
